'''''UFC 297: Strickland vs. du Plessis''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on January 20, 2024, at [[Scotiabank Arena]] in [[Toronto, Ontario]], Canada.<ref name="Toronto">{{cite web |last=Davidson |first=Neil |date=November 6, 2023 |title=Middleweight title on the line as UFC returns to Toronto with January card |url=https://www.thestar.com/sports/boxing-mma/middleweight-title-on-the-line-as-ufc-returns-to-toronto-with-january-card/article_93a9d401-8782-5934-aefb-7b6f6b94bfef.html |access-date=November 9, 2023 |website=thestar.com |archive-date=November 20, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231120213707/https://www.thestar.com/sports/boxing-mma/middleweight-title-on-the-line-as-ufc-returns-to-toronto-with-january-card/article_93a9d401-8782-5934-aefb-7b6f6b94bfef.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's seventh visit to Toronto and first since [[UFC 231]] in December 2018.<ref name="Toronto"/>

A [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Sean Strickland]] and former [[Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki#Welterweight championship|KSW Welterweight Champion]] [[Dricus du Plessis]] headlined the event.<ref name="Main">{{cite web |last=Leonard |first=Harvey |date=November 6, 2023 |title=Sean Strickland's first title defense announced for UFC 297, Khamzat Chimaev misses out |url=https://www.mmanews.com/.amp/news/sean-strickland-title-defense-announced-ufc-297-khamzat-chimaev-misses-out |access-date=November 6, 2023 |website=mmanews.com |archive-date=November 6, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231106201316/https://www.mmanews.com/.amp/news/sean-strickland-title-defense-announced-ufc-297-khamzat-chimaev-misses-out |url-status=live }}</ref>

A [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship]] bout for the vacant title between former title challenger [[Raquel Pennington]] and [[Mayra Bueno Silva]] took place at the event.<ref name="Vacant">{{cite web |last=Calhoun |first=Curtis |date=November 3, 2023 |title=UFC 297: Raquel Pennington vs. Mayra Bueno Silva booked for vacant UFC bantamweight title |url=https://www.mmanews.com/.amp/news/ufc-pennington-mayra-bueno-silva-booked-vacant-title |access-date=November 3, 2023 |website=mmanews.com |archive-date=November 4, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231104011057/https://www.mmanews.com/.amp/news/ufc-pennington-mayra-bueno-silva-booked-vacant-title |url-status=live }}</ref>  The title was vacated when former champion [[Amanda Nunes]] announced her retirement immediately after defending her title at [[UFC 289]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Mahjouri |first=Shakiel |date=June 11, 2023 |title=UFC 289 results, highlights: Amanda Nunes announces sudden retirement after dominant win over Irene Aldana |url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-289-results-highlights-amanda-nunes-announces-sudden-retirement-after-dominant-win-over-irene-aldana/live/ |access-date=November 3, 2023 |website=cbssports.com |archive-date=July 17, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230717151930/https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-289-results-highlights-amanda-nunes-announces-sudden-retirement-after-dominant-win-over-irene-aldana/live/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

A [[UFC Featherweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Alexander Volkanovski]] and [[Ilia Topuria]] was expected to take place at this event.<ref>{{cite web |last=Cain |first=Jeff |date=October 9, 2023 |title=Alexander Volkanovski vs. Ilia Topuria set for UFC 297 |url=https://www.mmaweekly.com/news/alexander-volkanovski-vs-ilia-topuria-set-for-ufc-297 |access-date=November 3, 2023 |website=mmaweekly.com |archive-date=October 10, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231010140529/https://www.mmaweekly.com/news/alexander-volkanovski-vs-ilia-topuria-set-for-ufc-297 |url-status=live }}</ref> However, Volkanovski was instead pulled to face [[Islam Makhachev]] for the [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] at [[UFC 294]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Martin |first=Damon |date=October 10, 2023 |title=Charles Oliveira out; Alexander Volkanovski now faces Islam Makhachev in UFC 294 main event |url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/10/10/23912292/charles-oliveira-out-alexander-volkanovski-now-faces-islam-makhachev-in-ufc-294-main-event |access-date=November 6, 2023 |website=mmafighting.com |archive-date=October 11, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231011093653/https://www.mmafighting.com/platform/amp/2023/10/10/23912292/charles-oliveira-out-alexander-volkanovski-now-faces-islam-makhachev-in-ufc-294-main-event |url-status=live }}</ref> As a result, this fight was pushed back one month to headline [[UFC 298]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Faria |first=Colby |date=November 6, 2023 |title=Alexander Volkanovski vs. Ilia Topuria featherweight title bout headlines UFC 298 |url=https://www.mmanews.com/.amp/news/alexander-volkanovski-ilia-topuria-title-headlines-ufc-298 |access-date=November 6, 2023 |website=mmanews.com |archive-date=November 6, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231106230709/https://www.mmanews.com/.amp/news/alexander-volkanovski-ilia-topuria-title-headlines-ufc-298 |url-status=live }}</ref>

A light heavyweight rematch between former [[Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki#Light heavyweight championship|KSW]] and [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship|UFC Light Heavyweight Champion]] [[Jan Błachowicz]] and [[Aleksandar Rakić]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{cite web |date=November 11, 2023 |title=Jan Blachowicz vs. Aleksandar Rakic 2 officially booked for UFC 297 in Toronto |url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/11/ufc-297-jan-blachowicz-vs-aleksandar-rakic-2-officially-booked |access-date=December 7, 2023 |website=mmajunkie.usatoday.com |archive-date=November 18, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231118041421/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/11/ufc-297-jan-blachowicz-vs-aleksandar-rakic-2-officially-booked |url-status=live }}</ref> The pairing previously met at [[UFC on ESPN: Błachowicz vs. Rakić]] where Blachowicz won the fight after Rakić was rendered unable to continue due to a knee injury.<ref>{{cite web |last=Doherty |first=Dan |date=May 15, 2022 |title=UFC Vegas 54: Aleksandar Rakic injures knee, falls to Jan Blachowicz |url=https://cagesidepress.com/2022/05/15/ufc-vegas-54-jan-blachowicz-aleksandar-rakic/ |access-date=December 7, 2023 |website=cagesidepress.com |archive-date=May 15, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220515045239/https://cagesidepress.com/2022/05/15/ufc-vegas-54-jan-blachowicz-aleksandar-rakic/ |url-status=live }}</ref> However, Błachowicz withdrew due to a shoulder injury and the bout was scrapped.<ref name="Jan out">{{cite web |last=King |first=Nolan |date=December 7, 2023 |title=Jan Blachowicz announces surgery, issues emotional statement on UFC 297 withdrawal |url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-297-jan-blachowicz-shoulder-surgery-withdrawal-aleksandar-rakic |access-date=December 7, 2023 |website=mmajunkie.usatoday.com |archive-date=December 7, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231207193547/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-297-jan-blachowicz-shoulder-surgery-withdrawal-aleksandar-rakic |url-status=live }}</ref>

A light heavyweight bout between former title challenger [[Dominick Reyes]] and [[Carlos Ulberg]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{cite web |last=Hannoun |first=Farah |date=November 13, 2023 |title=Carlos Ulberg gets wish, meets skidding Dominick Reyes at UFC 297 on Jan. 20 |url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/11/ufc-297-news-carlos-ulberg-vs-dominick-reyes-mma-fight-booking-matchup-announcement |access-date=November 13, 2023 |website=mmajunkie.usatoday.com |archive-date=November 14, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231114015944/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/11/ufc-297-news-carlos-ulberg-vs-dominick-reyes-mma-fight-booking-matchup-announcement |url-status=live }}</ref> However, in late December 2023, it was announced that match was off due to an injury sustained by Ulberg.<ref>{{cite web |last=Bohn |first=Mike |date=December 30, 2023 |title='Frustrated' Dominick Reyes says Carlos Ulberg out of UFC 297 due to injury |url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-297-news-dominick-reyes-says-carlos-ulberg-out-injury-toronto-replacement |access-date=December 30, 2023 |website=mmajunkie.usatoday.com |archive-date=December 31, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231231032358/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-297-news-dominick-reyes-says-carlos-ulberg-out-injury-toronto-replacement |url-status=live }}</ref> They were rescheduled for [[UFC Fight Night: Brady vs. Luque]] two months later.<ref>{{cite web |date=January 9, 2024 |title=Dominick Reyes vs. Carlos Ulberg rebooked for UFC Atlantic City in March |url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/01/ufc-fight-night-dominick-reyes-vs-carlos-ulberg-rebooked-atlantic-city |access-date=January 9, 2024 |website=mmajunkie.usatoday.com |archive-date=January 9, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240109230446/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/01/ufc-fight-night-dominick-reyes-vs-carlos-ulberg-rebooked-atlantic-city |url-status=live }}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, Ramon Taveras and [[Malcolm Gordon (fighter)|Malcolm Gordon]] missed weight. Taveras weighed in at 139.75 pounds, three and three quarters pounds over the bantamweight non-title fight limit. Gordon weighed in at 127.5 pounds, one and a half pounds over the flyweight non-title fight limit. Both bouts proceeded at catchweight with Taveras forfeiting 30% and Gordon 20% of their purses, which went to their opponents Serhiy Sidey and Jimmy Flick respectively.<ref>{{cite web |date=January 19, 2024 |title=UFC 297: Strickland vs. Du Plessis weigh-in results |url=https://cagesidepress.com/2024/01/19/ufc-297-strickland-vs-du-plessis-weigh-in-results/ |access-date=January 19, 2024 |website=cagesidepress.com |archive-date=January 19, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240119111616/https://cagesidepress.com/2024/01/19/ufc-297-strickland-vs-du-plessis-weigh-in-results/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

Prior to the event, UFC president [[Dana White]] announced that the event had set new records for the highest grossing arena gate in UFC history and Canada.<ref>{{cite web |last=Reinsmith |first=Trent |date=January 19, 2024 |title=UFC 297 Breaks Canadian Live Gate Record For Arena Event |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/trentreinsmith/2024/01/19/ufc-297-breaks-canadian-live-gate-record-for-arena-event/ |access-date=January 21, 2024 |website=Forbes |language=en |archive-date=January 20, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240120030537/https://www.forbes.com/sites/trentreinsmith/2024/01/19/ufc-297-breaks-canadian-live-gate-record-for-arena-event/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, former [[UFC Lightweight Championship|UFC Lightweight Champion]] [[Frankie Edgar]] was announced as the next "modern wing" [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee during June's International Fight Week festivities in Las Vegas.<ref>{{cite web |last=Bohn |first=Mike |date=January 20, 2024 |title=Frankie Edgar announced for 2024 UFC Hall of Fame class |url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/01/ufc-hall-of-fame-frankie-edgar-announced-2024-class-international-fight-week |access-date=January 21, 2024 |website=mmajunkie.usatoday.com |archive-date=January 22, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240122011636/https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/01/ufc-hall-of-fame-frankie-edgar-announced-2024-class-international-fight-week |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web |last=Mazique |first=Brian |date=January 21, 2024 |title=UFC 297 results - winners, finishes, bonuses and highlights |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/brianmazique/2024/01/21/ufc-297-results-winners-finishes-bonuses-and-highlights/?sh=28d7ddbf6c7f |access-date=January 21, 2024 |website=forbes.com |archive-date=February 7, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240207134855/https://www.forbes.com/sites/brianmazique/2024/01/21/ufc-297-results-winners-finishes-bonuses-and-highlights/?sh=28d7ddbf6c7f |url-status=live }}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Dricus du Plessis vs. Sean Strickland'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Gillian Robertson''' and '''Jasmine Jasudavicius'''
